_meta.description: Authored range-reading scenarios. The user multi-selects from `options`; `correctAnswers` is the subset that belongs in the answer.

question.prompt: You bet 1/3 pot with top pair. Which worse hands can call you?
question.explanation: Worse hands that call are weaker queens and draws (pairs + flush/straight draws). AA/KK are overpairs and sets are better than your top pair - they raise, they don't call as 'worse'.

scenarios.title: BB check-raises a dry king-high flop

question.prompt: BB check-raises your c-bet. Which hands make up a sensible check-raise range here?
question.explanation: A balanced check-raise range = value (sets 88/22, two pair K8s) plus a few bluffs with backdoor equity (76s, JT). A2s and unpaired KJ are typically just calls, not raises.

question.prompt: Which hands make up the Small Blind's value 3-bet range?
question.explanation: The value portion of a 3-bet range is premium pairs and big aces. A5s is a bluff (not value), and 72o/K9o are simply folds preflop.

question.prompt: The turn brings a straight card and BB leads out. Which better hands are now ahead of your overpair?
question.explanation: On 9-8-5-7 many straights (T6, JT, 6x, T6) and two pair/sets now beat aces. Flush draws and AK high are still behind - they are not 'better hands'.

question.prompt: You have top pair good kicker on the river. Which worse hands call a half-pot value bet?
question.explanation: Worse hands that call are weaker queens and pairs (QJ/QT/A7/K7). Sets (77/22) and AA are better than you - they raise or are already ahead, so they are not part of 'worse hands that call'.
